title: Savory Crunchy Argentinian Pork Tenderloin in Preserved Lemons Banana-Leaf
description: This savory and crunchy meal is perfect for a quick lunch or dinner. The pork tenderloin is cooked using fast-action yeast to keep it low-glycemic-friendly, then sashimi-cut for easy portioning.
instructions: Cut the pork into sashimi slices, about 1/4 inch thick.
Mix preserved lemons with olive oil and seasonings. Place sliced pork in a single layer on banana leaves lined baking sheet.
Place mixture over top of pork slices, ensuring they are fully covered. Arrange lemon halves on top for extra flavoring.
Place baking sheet in preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes. Rotate the sheet halfway through cooking time to ensure even browning.
Remove from oven, let cool slightly before serving. Serve with a side of sweet potato mash or quinoa salad.
Dust slices with additional kosher salt and pepper just before serving if desired.
